title: Adopt br as the project task tracker
description: Add tool-br skill and enforce beads issue id in commit messages via a standalone git hook.
close_reason: tool-br skill and beads-commit-msg hook added and committed in 0220a35.

title: Rewrite core AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions.md fragments for genericity and signal
description: Researched external agent-instruction repos (lattice, gastownhall/beads, JuliusBrussee skills, mattpocock/skills, ponytail) and redesigned the .basicly/core/fragments catalog: moved repo-specific content (project-defaults, python-style) into .basicly-local overlay, rewrote weak fragments (core-rules, quality-gate, if-stuck->decision-protocol, no-user-path-leakage->secure-coding) with concrete, high-signal guidance, added new generic fragments (knowledge-priming, session-completion, non-interactive-shell). Also fixed a real bug: applyTo in generated copilot instructions must be a string not a YAML array. Removed redundant source-fragments comment from generated file headers (context poisoning with no tooling use).
close_reason: Fragment catalog redesigned and committed in 0220a35: core fragments genericized, weak content rewritten, applyTo bug fixed.

title: Support Conventional Commits breaking-change marker and add conventional-commits skill
description: commit-msg.py rejected the spec's '!' breaking-change indicator (feat!:, feat(scope)!:) with no way to express it. Added optional '!' support before the colon, shifted description group index, added regression tests. Also added .basicly/core/skills/conventional-commits/SKILL.md (with skill-creator-conformant Inputs/Outputs sections and trigger-quality validation) since no existing skill taught the full commit message format proactively before the hooks reject it.
close_reason: Breaking-change '!' support, conventional-commits skill, and clarified lowercase-description wording all committed (404adab, 25f4e7b).

title: test_cli.py mutates real repo generated-manifest.json instead of using isolated copies
description: run_basicly() ran build/skills-build/check against the real REPO_ROOT for 6 tests instead of an isolated tmp copy, rewriting .basicly/generated-manifest.json's timestamp on every test run and dirtying the working tree after every pytest invocation (including pre-push). Fixed by adding a work_repo tmp_path fixture and routing all CLI invocations through an isolated copy, consolidating the 3 tests that already did this manually via local copytree calls.
close_reason: Fixed via work_repo tmp_path fixture in tests/test_cli.py (727af06).

title: harden commit-msg description rules and add self-improvement retro fragment
description: Two fixes from this session's real mistakes: (1) commit-msg.py and the conventional-commits skill said description must 'start lowercase' but the actual rule requires the whole description to be lowercase with no underscores -- both real rejections I hit. Clarified wording + added concrete invalid examples in both places. (2) Added project/self-improvement-retro fragment: at session end, if a real rejection/mistake occurred, propose the exact fragment/skill/hook wording fix that would have prevented it, present for explicit approval, never self-apply. Also deduped two now-redundant bullets (core-rules secrets/validation line duplicated by the broadened secure-coding fragment) to stay under the copilot 6000-char budget.

title: fix ruff I001 import-sort violation masked by stale local .ruff_cache
description: GH Actions quality-gates failed ruff check on tests/test_loader.py and tests/test_skills.py (I001: missing blank line between third-party and first-party import groups). Every local ruff check this session reported clean because a stale .ruff_cache (gitignored, not present in CI's fresh checkout) was serving a false-clean cached result for these two files. Fixed the actual import formatting with ruff --fix after clearing the cache; verified clean with cache cleared.

title: fold beads close into the resolving commit instead of a trailing chore commit
description: tool-br skill said to close issues 'when finished' without specifying commit sequencing, so I was closing after committing the fix, producing a separate near-empty chore: close <id> commit every time (e.g. 08e5e6a had zero code value). Updated Safe Defaults/Common Pitfalls to close before the resolving commit and fold .beads/issues.jsonl into it; amend if forgotten and unpushed, accept a trailing commit only if already pushed.
close_reason: tool-br skill updated to close-before-commit sequencing; this very commit demonstrates it.

title: basicly v0.1 release: uvx-installable, consumer-ready
description: Umbrella epic for the initial distributable release of basicly (arch docs/architecture.md §9). Goal: a fresh consumer repo (e.g. terminal) can run 'uvx --from git+... basicly init' then build/skills-build/hooks-build to get working AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions.md, skills, and git hooks. Success = both halves (guidance + gates) install cleanly from a pinned package. Children track the roadmap gaps in §11.

title: Move jinja2 from dev group to [project.dependencies]
description: §11.2: renderers/common.py imports jinja2 at runtime but jinja2 is only in [dependency-groups].dev. An installed wheel would crash at build time. Move jinja2 (and any other runtime import currently dev-only) into [project.dependencies]; verify nothing runtime-imported remains dev-only.
close_reason: Moved jinja2 from [dependency-groups].dev to [project.dependencies]; uv.lock now lists it as a runtime dep of basicly; import smoke test passes.

title: Enable packaging: add [build-system], flip tool.uv.package, keep console entry point
description: §11.1: add a [build-system] table (hatchling or equivalent), remove/flip tool.uv.package=false so the src-layout package builds. [project.scripts] basicly=basicly.cli:main already exists — confirm it resolves from a built wheel.

title: Bundle the core catalog as package data so init can materialize it
description: §4.1/§11.6: .basicly/core (fragments, skills, hooks, targets, templates) lives outside src/ and is NOT in the wheel by default. A fresh consumer install has no catalog. Ship the catalog inside the package (e.g. src/basicly/catalog/ or force-include) so 'basicly init' can copy it onto a consumer's disk. Keep this repo's dogfooded .basicly/core as the authoring source; define the source->packaged sync story.

title: Verify uvx install end-to-end from a clean environment
description: §9 quality gate: from a scratch dir, run 'uvx --from git+<repo>@<ref> basicly --help' and 'basicly list/build' against a materialized catalog; confirm the entry point resolves, jinja2 is present, and the catalog is found. Documents the real consumer install command.

title: Implement 'basicly init' (basicly.toml + overlay skeleton + materialize core catalog)
description: §8/§11.8: scaffold basicly.toml [paths], create .basicly-local/fragments/user/, and materialize .basicly/core from the bundled package catalog (depends on catalog bundling). Idempotent, never overwrites existing overlay content. Print next-steps (build / skills-build / hooks-build).

title: Add install provenance/state tracking (.basicly/state/install.json)
description: §9/§11.8: record installed catalog version + provenance so 'update' can reason about upgrades and 'check' can report drift. [Planned].

title: Implement 'basicly hooks-build' (project core/hooks into consumer pre-commit wiring)
description: §11.6: project core/hooks/*.py + generate/merge .pre-commit-config.yaml entries (and/or .git/hooks) into a consumer repo. Write only changed bytes, update manifest, matching 'build' semantics. Must not clobber unrelated pre-commit hooks the consumer already has.

title: Implement 'basicly hooks-check' (staleness parity for projected hooks)
description: §11.6: byte-for-byte staleness check of projected hooks + wiring vs the catalog, exit 1 on mismatch, no auto-fix — same contract as 'check' / 'skills-check'.

title: Implement override validation (replaces exists / override:true required / no mutual user-user replaces)
description: §5/§11.3: planner._apply_user_replacements removes core fragments listed in 'replaces' but never validates them. Enforce: replaces targets must exist; override:true required before a replacement is honored; reject two user fragments that replace each other. Hard errors, with tests.

title: Add 'enforced_by' schema field + enforcement-pointer lint (§3.1)
description: §3.1/§11.4: context minimalism has no mechanical check. Add an 'enforced_by' field to the fragment schema and a lint that flags prose restating a mechanically-enforced rule instead of pointing at the command/config.

title: Implement 'basicly verify' + 'basicly build --verify'
description: §6/§11.5: standalone deterministic gate beyond schema/duplicate-id (duplicate/near-duplicate bodies, static-dictionary contradiction, vague-phrase ambiguity, scope-overlap), plus override validation and the enforced_by check. 'build --verify' runs verify first and writes nothing on failure. Depends on D1 + D2.

title: Implement 'basicly review' (agent-assisted semantic review, advisory)
description: §6/§11.5: advisory semantic pass where an agent reads the rendered diff for contradiction/ambiguity. Report, not a merge gate. Post-MVP; depends on the deterministic 'verify' existing first.

title: Design + implement Python skill source schema and loader change
description: §11.7: define the structured Python (non-SKILL.md) source format, update schema + skills loader to read it. Migrate existing core/skills/*/SKILL.md sources. Half of a schema+loader+projector change.

title: Implement skill projector: emit SKILL.md from the new source format
description: §11.7: update skills-build/skills-check to project the new Python source into SKILL.md at target roots only, so the discoverable copy exists exactly once. Depends on E1.

title: curl bootstrap shim for consumers without uv/Python
description: §9/§11.8: thin curl|sh shim over the same basicly commands for consumers lacking uv/Python. [Planned].

title: Catalog selection / flavors (install fragments+hooks but not skills)
description: §9/§11.8: let a consumer choose a subset of the catalog at install time. [Planned].

title: Codex scoped rules (.codex/rules/*.rules) renderer
description: §4.4/§11.8: Codex currently gets only the shared AGENTS.md baseline; scoped rules are [Deferred].

title: Cursor target renderer + templates
description: §11.9: Cursor as a target is [Deferred] — no renderer, no templates yet.

title: Packaging & distribution: make basicly uvx-installable
description: §9 / §11.1-11.2: packaging is disabled (no [build-system] table, tool.uv.package=false, catalog lives outside src/). Until fixed 'uvx --from git+... basicly' cannot resolve basicly.cli. Makes the package buildable/installable and the catalog shippable.

title: basicly init: scaffold & materialize catalog in a consumer repo
description: §8/§11.8: 'basicly init' is [Planned]. A real consumer needs an explicit command that lays down basicly.toml, the overlay skeleton, and the managed core catalog materialized from the installed package.

title: Ship the gates half: hooks-build / hooks-check projection
description: §11.6: core/hooks/*.py are a dogfooded catalog location but a fresh consumer has no command to install them into .git/hooks / .pre-commit-config.yaml. Without this the deterministic 'gates' half (§0) can't be installed. Mirrors the build/check contract.

title: Catalog integrity: override validation & verification pipeline
description: §5/§6/§11.3-11.5: extension mechanism and deterministic verification are only partly built. Harden correctness so a consumer's overrides and catalog can't silently break.

title: Migrate skill source format from SKILL.md to a non-discoverable format
description: §4.2/§11.7: catalog skills authored as SKILL.md risk double-loading. Decided fix: author skills in a non-SKILL.md format and project to SKILL.md only at target roots.

title: Post-release distribution backlog (deferred in §11.8-11.9)
description: Captured so nothing is lost; explicitly out of scope for v0.1 per architecture §11.8-11.9.
